chunks.text: Page 1 of 2 ORDER SHEET HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD C.P No.D-298 of 2026. DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE For orders on M.A No.1337/26. For orders on office objections. For orders on M.A No.1338/26. For hearing of main case. 24.02.2026. Mr. Muhammad Hashim Bajeer, Advocate for Petitioner. ***************************************** At the very outset, learned counsel for the petitioner submits that the petitioner filed a Revenue Appeal before the Additional Deputy Commissioner, Badin in the year, 2024, which has remained pending without adjudication. He states that upon filing of the said appeal, the Additional Deputy Commissioner issued notices and also called for reports from the concerned Mukhtiarkar as well as the Assistant Commissioner. In addition, notices were also issued to the private respondents. However, despite issuance of notices, receipt of reports from the Revenue authorities and the lapse of considerable time, Respondent No.2 has failed to decide the Revenue Appeal in accordance with law. Mr.Muhammad Ismail Bhutto, learned Additional A.G Sindh, who is present in Court in other matters, waives notice of this petition. After examining the petition and its annexures, he submits that if a Revenue Appeal is pending adjudication before Respondent No.2, the same shall be decided strictly in accordance with law after providing proper opportunity of hearing to both parties. At this stage, learned counsel for the petitioner expresses satisfaction with the statement and seeks disposal of the petition. In view of the above, the instant petition along with pending application (s), stands disposed of with directions to Respondent No.2, the Additional Deputy Commissioner, Badin to decide the Revenue Appeal earlier filed by the

At this stage, learned counsel for the petitioner expresses satisfaction with the statement and seeks disposal of the petition. In view of the above, the instant petition along with pending application (s), stands disposed of with directions to Respondent No.2, the Additional Deputy Commissioner, Badin to decide the Revenue Appeal earlier filed by the Page 2 of 2 petitioner within sixty days, strictly in accordance with law and after providing proper opportunity of hearing to both sides. Let a copy of this order be communicated to the Deputy Commissioner, Badin, as well as the Additional Deputy Commissioner, Badin, for compliance. JUDGE JUDGE Ali.
